holdings.note: SpaceX — IPO'd Jun 2026; FMP may not yet cover earnings/dividend dates for a brand-new listing.

macro.label: Triple witching
macro.detail: Quarterly simultaneous expiry of index futures, index options & single-stock options — volume/volatility spike and pinning risk in the most options-heavy names.

macro.label: Triple witching
macro.detail: Quarterly simultaneous expiry of index futures, index options & single-stock options — volume/volatility spike and pinning risk in the most options-heavy names.

INTC.read: INTC is up 10.5% today and 14.4% on the week into a price level (≈$134) that looks stretched for a name with no dated company catalyst on the board — no confirmed earnings, no ex-div in the window provided. With today being triple witching, a chunk of this move could be options-driven gamma/positioning rather than fundamental, which is exactly the kind of strength to fade partially rather than chase.
INTC.bull: Momentum + short-covering into expiry can extend the squeeze, and any leaked foundry/AI customer news would re-rate the name higher from here.
INTC.bear: Post-expiry gamma unwind plus no dated catalyst to defend the move means the easiest path is a give-back next week.

NVDA.read: No dated company catalyst on the tape for NVDA right now — next earnings isn't scheduled in the feed, so there's no binary window to lean into. Price is firm (+2.8% on the day, +2.7% on the week at $210), which is constructive but not stretched enough to force a trim absent a known event. Today's triple witching can amplify intraday noise into the close; treat any post-expiry gap as signal, not setup.
NVDA.bull: Trend is intact and tape is bid — no catalyst means no reason to interrupt a working position.
NVDA.bear: Without a dated print to anchor to, a sentiment or AI-spend headline can swing the name in either direction unhedged.

ORCL.read: No company-specific dated catalyst on the board for ORCL right now — just triple witching today and again in September, which is positioning noise, not a fundamental driver. Price is barely moved on the day (+0.43%) and flat on the week (+0.17%), so there's no stretched move to fade and no washout to lean into. The actionable window will open once the next FQ1 earnings date gets confirmed (typically mid-September), where AI/OCI backlog and capex guide will set the tone.
ORCL.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (no ORCL-specific catalyst dated; earnings TBD)
ORCL.bull: OCI/AI backlog continues to compound and the next print re-rates the multiple higher on raised capex absorption.
ORCL.bear: Hyperscaler capex digestion or a soft RPO print into Sept witching unwinds the AI-premium multiple.

NOK.read: NOK is down 4.3% on the week with no company-specific dated catalyst on the board — no confirmed earnings or ex-div in the feed, just triple witching today which is a flow event, not a fundamental one. Without a near-term print to anchor an add or trim window, this is a wait-for-the-catalyst name; the weakness isn't deep enough to force action and there's no event to trim into. Next real signal will be the Q2 print when it gets scheduled.
NOK.bull: Networks/AI infra exposure plus dividend support; a confirmed earnings date with stable guide opens an add window.
NOK.bear: No dated catalyst means drift risk — the -4.3% week can extend without an event to reset sentiment.

NBIS.read: NBIS is up 29% in five sessions with no dated company catalyst on the board — that's a momentum-driven extension, not a fundamentally re-rated print. Triple witching today (2026-06-19) can amplify both directions through dealer hedging in a name this volatile, so the asymmetric window is into strength rather than chasing. Without an earnings or guidance event tethering the move, the burden of proof shifts to price holding the breakout after expiry flows clear.
NBIS.bull: AI-infra momentum + post-expiry positioning reset could keep the squeeze going if buyers absorb the unwind.
NBIS.bear: +29% in a week with no dated catalyst leaves the name exposed to a gamma unwind once today's expiry prints.

KO.read: Tiny 5-share KO position is down 3.2% on the week with no company-specific dated catalysts on the board — no confirmed earnings, no ex-div in the feed — so there's no event-driven window to lean into here. Today is triple witching, which can amp up index-level noise in a defensive name like KO but isn't a fundamental catalyst. Without a dated print or dividend date to anchor an add/trim window, the honest call is to wait for the next earnings or ex-div to populate before acting.
KO.next_catalyst: 2026-06-19 — Triple witching (macro only; no KO-specific catalyst on calendar)
KO.bull: Defensive staple pulling back into a quad-witch — a bounce off support with a dated ex-div or Q2 print would give a real add window.
KO.bear: No company catalyst means the -3% drift can extend on macro/tape pressure with nothing scheduled to break the slide.

SPCX.read: Brand-new June 2026 IPO with no confirmed earnings or dividend dates in the feed yet — there's no dated company catalyst to trade around, just today's triple witching which can amplify intraday noise in a thin, freshly-listed name. The first real window will be the IPO lockup expiry (typically ~180 days, so roughly Dec 2026) and the debut earnings print; until one of those gets a confirmed date, this is a watch, not an add or trim.
SPCX.next_catalyst: 2026-06-19 — Triple witching (only dated event on the tape; no SPCX-specific catalyst confirmed yet)
SPCX.bull: Scarcity name with rare private-market exposure now public; pre-lockup float is tight and any confirmed first-print date could re-rate the stock.
SPCX.bear: No earnings history, no confirmed catalysts, and a ~180-day lockup overhang looming into late 2026 that can cap upside well before the first print.

disclaimer: Forward earnings dates are third-party aggregator estimates, not company-confirmed — confirm against the company's IR release (~2 weeks ahead). Dividends, macro prints and witching are scheduled/confirmed. Not investment advice.
